content:
@Alec Leamas @Dave Register
On some rpi bookworm and trixe I get the below error when build from master. Since CL builds works it might be a difference what deps to use in file INSTALL compared to what the cl builds use?

LU.so libs/pugixml/libOCPN_PUGIXML.a libs/o_sound/lib_SOUND.a -L/usr/lib/arm-linux-gnueabihf -pthread /usr/lib/arm-linux-gnueabihf/libportaudio.so -lwx_gtk3u_gl-3.2 -lwx_baseu_net-3.2 -lwx_baseu_xml-3.2 -lwx_gtk3u_html-3.2 -lwx_gtk3u_aui-3.2 -lwx_gtk3u_core-3.2 -lwx_baseu-3.2 -lwx_gtk3u_richtext-3.2 -lwx_gtk3u_webview-3.2 libs/wxservdisc/libWXSERVDISC.a libs/gdal/libGDAL.a /usr/lib/arm-linux-gnueabihf/libGLEW.so

/usr/bin/ld: CMakeFiles/opencpn.dir/gui/src/connections_dlg.cpp.o: in function `GeneralPanel::PrioritiesBtn::PrioritiesBtn(wxWindow*)::{lambda(wxCommandEvent&)#1}::operator()(wxCommandEvent&) const':
/home/rpi500/Builds/OCPN/OpenCPN/gui/src/connections_dlg.cpp:803: undefined reference to `PriorityDlg::PriorityDlg(wxWindow*)'

....
gIns(wxGLContext*, ViewPort const&, int, int)'

make[2]: *** [CMakeFiles/opencpn.dir/build.make:2373: opencpn] Error 1
make[1]: *** [CMakeFiles/Makefile2:1005: CMakeFiles/opencpn.dir/all] Error 2
make[1]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
[ 75%] Built target tests
make: *** [Makefile:159: all] Error 2
rpi500@raspberrypi:~/Builds/OCPN/OpenCPN/build $

This is not about dependencies, all missing symbols are from the main program.

And these do certainly exist. Have you tried removing the build directory and rebuild from scratch?

Hmm.. I did deleted the "root" of build. But now when the complete /build was deleted a new build works fine.
Very good. Thanks for the hint. ( This remind me of the old fashion Win: Ctr-Alt-Del. :) )

I have occasionally run into the same mess. AFAICT it's about cmake's generated dependeny chains which governs the link ordering being confused by structural changes in different git versions, for example when doing bisects.

So I'll try this whenever a build obstacle.
This is new Rpi500 with a preinstalled SD card. I get no external plugins by a catalog master update?
Any hints for that as well?
.. When he boots he say's Rasbian 32 b. Don't we support aarch64? I may change to Openplotter that's 64 b?
rpi500@raspberrypi:~ $ uname -m
aarch64
rpi500@raspberrypi:~ $ cat /etc/os-release
PRETTY_NAME="Raspbian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
NAME="Raspbian GNU/Linux"
VERSION_ID="12"
VERSION="12 (bookworm)"
VERSION_CODENAME=bookworm
ID=raspbian
ID_LIKE=debian
rpi500@raspberrypi:~ $
From log file:

16:39:04.243 MESSAGE ocpn_app.cpp:885 wxWidgets version: wxWidgets 3.2.2 Linux 64 bit wxGTK
16:39:04.246 MESSAGE ocpn_app.cpp:890 OS Version reports as:  6.12
16:39:04.246 MESSAGE ocpn_app.cpp:892 MemoryStatus:  mem_total: 7951 mb,  mem_initial: 434 mb
16:39:04.246 MESSAGE ocpn_app.cpp:904 OCPN_OSDetail:  armhf ; Raspbian GNU/Linux ; 12 ; raspbian ; debian

.. When he boots he say's Rasbian 32 b.

He? Must be a she, no doubt.

If it says it is 32 bits when booting you have basically installed the wrong OS, sorry. We do support aarch64 and only that on RPi these days.

What does the command uname -asay?

Uname -m say's as of above "aarch64".
Nevertheless, this 32 bit Bookworm OS was as mentioned installed on the SD card provided when the Rpi500 was delivered today, so not "my" installation. The reason for this 32 bit OS I can't guess. The card was labeled "Rasbian 32 Gb(size)".
But now she, he (or hen?) is running 64 b trixie. Really fast and no plugin obstacles.
The intention is this will be my new boat Nav-OS.
Thanks for your support.
